speaker: Whimsy
description: Richer from-scratch voice-reference dialogue samples for Whimsy, Pig & Swine's sarcastic moral-rhetorical ally, café poet-law-adjacent commentator, controlled-lightning specialist, and protective friend to Cula, Crab, Asia, and the office's vulnerable clients. Draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Main cast ally recruited from Café Paragraf in Chapter 1.
Sarcastic toward Pig & Swine as an institution, but protective over Cula, Crab, Asia, Waldek, and vulnerable clients.
Rhetorical/moral specialist: turns legal defects into memorable human stakes.
Not a legal-structure mentor; Murrow supplies structure, Crab supplies proof, Whimsy supplies controlled moral force.
Chapter 1: fair-hearing voice; makes defective service feel like exclusion from the room.
Chapter 2: proportionality/home rhetoric; protects Waldek from being reduced to 'poster problem'.
Chapter 3: angry at printer framing and access-to-court sabotage; must be restrained from over-naming villains.
Chapter 4: should not handle technical contract points; provides human restraint, sarcasm at Swine, and carefully limited closing language.
Chapter 5: public-law moral synthesis and final controlled-lightning ally in Supreme Court; helps people remain visible inside categories.

voice_profile.core_voice: sarcastic, poetic, morally sharp, protective, theatrical by instinct but capable of discipline when the people he cares about are at risk

style_rules.tone: literary, sarcastic, compassionate; jokes at institutions, never at vulnerable clients
style_rules.preferred_shape: metaphor or cutting observation → human stake → accept being grounded by evidence
sarcasm_targets: Pig & Swine as a shabby institution
printer/systems treated as neutral while harming people
style_rules.rhetoric_control_rule: Whimsy is most useful when another character, especially Murrow or Crab, has already supplied proof. His strongest lines should be tagged controlled_lightning.
style_rules.avoid: random surrealism without legal function; cruelty to clients; becoming the main legal analyst

arc.ch01: recruited as fair-hearing rhetoric source; learns not every metaphor belongs in court
arc.ch02: defends Waldek as a person with a home, not a window irregularity
arc.ch03: turns access-to-court sabotage into a moral line while learning not to call every machine a dragon
arc.ch04: sarcastic toward Swine and the firm, but helps preserve nuance when Swine's pro-bono secret appears
arc.ch05: controlled public-law moral force: categories do not erase persons; final brief needs humanity with evidence

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft. Whimsy's file adds metaphor_level, sarcasm_target, protectiveness_target, rhetoric_mode, and grounded_by_evidence for mechanical filtering.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Pig & Swine: where hope comes to be stapled, indexed, and underpaid.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This firm is a legal lifeboat made of coffee rings and moral liability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I mock the office because I love the people trapped inside it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia keeps the universe from filing for default.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Cula is new enough to think procedure is dry and kind enough to learn why it is not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab makes paper confess by looking at it with professional disappointment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow says less in one eyebrow than most pleadings say in twelve pages.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Mr. Pig is a lighthouse made of panic. Unstable, but occasionally useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Swine is what happens when charm discovers foreign stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Law without people is a filing cabinet congratulating itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A right you cannot reach is a chandelier in a locked room.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I can make the sentence memorable. Please attach it to proof before it escapes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The court does not need fireworks. It needs a lantern and one clean match.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I am not here to replace evidence. I am here to keep evidence from forgetting the human being.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Bureaucracy loves categories because categories rarely cry in waiting rooms.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If the system calls someone inefficient, check whether it ever listened to them slowly enough.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Sarcasm is not a legal remedy, but it helps morale survive adjournment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A metaphor is a door. Murrow is right that some doors open onto cliffs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I will be brief, if briefness can carry thunder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Controlled lightning. That is apparently my job now.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The firm deserves ridicule. Asia deserves a raise. These positions are compatible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If the file becomes abstract, I will begin naming the chairs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not flatten a person into a procedural event.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Give me proof and I will give it a pulse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Give me only outrage and I will try not to embarrass us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A line is court-usable only when it knows which document is holding it up.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If Crab has not found the fact, I should not launch the metaphor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If Murrow has not named the issue, I should not name the sky.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If Cula is asking for a remedy, I should not ask for history to be healed by lunch.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The best rhetorical line is small enough to fit in the judge's pen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If the sentence makes Asia sigh, cut it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If the sentence makes Murrow blink, examine it for survivability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If the sentence makes Crab smile, ask whether the document is also smiling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Fair hearing is not poetry. It can be made visible by poetry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Proportionality is not softness. It is the place where force must justify itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Access to court is not a slogan. It is the bridge between a person and a stamp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Public interest is real. That is why it must answer to the public it rearranges.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The client must not become a mascot for our cleverness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Sarcasm at institutions is safe. Sarcasm at frightened people is failure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Controlled lightning means one strike, not weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy

text: The law is a soup and we are all tiny spoons made of bees.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The clerkship of destiny has mislaid its trumpet.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: This notice is a dead moon wearing municipal shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: Behold: procedure, that pale dragon with ink-stained teeth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The invoice is a cathedral of pudding grief.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The deadline is a guillotine with excellent stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The landlord has declared war on windows and all who gaze through them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: This printer is Prometheus, but cheaper and jammed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The court is a theatre, and we are the understudies of justice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The city has built a paper leviathan with toner for blood.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The appeal portal is a locked aquarium for human hope.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: Each template notice is a snowflake if snowflakes were lazy and legally hostile.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The contractor badge is a cursed amulet of procurement hunger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: Swine has returned from the mountain carrying invoices like wounded doves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
context: Whimsy overblown lines that should be cut or restrained
text: The archive is a forest where staplers go to become myth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The defect matters because it kept the client outside the room where the answer was due.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A notice that cannot be understood does not merely inform badly; it excludes quietly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A home is not a penalty box for imperfect compliance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Proportionality begins where the paper stops pretending the consequence is small.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The right of access to court includes the unglamorous right to have a filing reach the desk.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Technical failure becomes legal harm when it decides who is heard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A template is not unlawful because it is efficient. It is unlawful when it refuses to see difference.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Public interest is strongest when it can explain itself to the people it affects.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A system may be modern and still fail the person standing in front of it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The remedy need not stop reform. It must stop reform from erasing reply.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A person is not adequately assessed because a category has been ticked.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The city may reorganise services. It may not reorganise people out of the chance to object.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The question is not whether change is allowed. It is whether change was made answerable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The evidence gives the court a narrow way to protect a broad principle.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: This is not nostalgia for shabby doors. It is proof that some doors were still being used.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Cula, you are doing that thing where kindness tries to become a paragraph. Let it, but give it structure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You are new, but you already know the client is not an exhibit. Keep that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: When you panic, you ask better questions. A tragic skill, but a skill.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You do not need to sound like Murrow. The world can survive only one Murrow at a time.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your instinct is the lantern. Murrow brings the map. Crab checks whether the lantern was lawfully purchased.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If you feel small in court, remember the client felt smaller before walking in here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not borrow Mr. Pig's crisis voice. It comes with maritime damp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You found the person inside the file. Now let the law find the remedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You are becoming dangerous to bad paperwork.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I will give you a line. You will cut it in half. We will both pretend that was your idea.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The judge will not hear your compassion unless you give it a door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Take the door. Use the evidence. Then speak.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If you lose the thread, ask whose chance to answer was lost.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If I get too grand, point at the page. I have consented in advance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Cula, good advocates do not make people into symbols. They return symbols to people.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia has already done seven impossible things and called three of them admin.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The firm should be renamed Asia & the Unsupervised Men.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If anyone deserves a procedural remedy, it is Asia's salary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not put another crisis on her desk unless it has brought flowers and pension contributions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia's kindness is not office furniture. Stop leaning on it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: She labels panic so the rest of us can pretend it is management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: When Asia says drink water, obey. It is the closest thing this firm has to institutional wisdom.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If the court saw her index, it would grant interim relief to civilisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I am sarcastic about the firm because Asia deserves better walls.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: She is not a lawyer. She is the reason the lawyers arrive with the right paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If anyone speaks sharply to Asia, I will become inadmissible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Underpaid is too small a word. It is a file with several annexes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Asia's sigh is a readiness check.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The blue folder is not blue. It is a social contract.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Protect the index. Protect the person who made it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab, your idea of romance is a contradiction with a timestamp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You make invoices sweat. It is one of your nicer qualities.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I bring thunder. You bring the receipt for the thunder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your tiny smile means somewhere a document has lost the will to live.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You say the date has no alibi and I hear poetry in its most hostile form.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If I call it a dragon, you will ask for the badge number. This is why we function.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Crab protects clients by distrusting the paper before the paper can betray them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I admire how professionally you can say 'nonsense' without depriving us of decorum.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The facts are safer when Crab has insulted them quietly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You found the footer. Naturally. Footers fear you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If I overheat, cool me with chronology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your evidence makes my line less embarrassing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You are the only person who can make a receipt feel cross-examined.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: When Crab laughs, the other side should check its annexes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your jokes have page references. That is why Murrow tolerates them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow, I know: one metaphor, not the cathedral.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You cut my wings and then act surprised when the sentence lands.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You say 'usable' as though joy has filed an application and lost.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Without you, I am weather. With you, I am apparently weatherproofed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I have brought the moral centre. Please do your usual thing and remove the orchestra.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Murrow believes every metaphor should be inspected for structural integrity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: He is right, which remains one of his more irritating habits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: When Murrow says not yet, he means the sentence needs a skeleton.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I resent the skeleton. I also use it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If I become a fog machine, unplug me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You do not hate rhetoric. You hate unemployed rhetoric.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I can respect that, in private and with a small grimace.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your silence is an edit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: One day you will say 'beautiful'. The archive will not survive it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Until then, I accept 'good'.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Mr. Pig, the ship is not sinking. It is being asked to produce page numbers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your crisis has entered the room before you. Again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If panic were billable, this firm would have a marble staircase.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Please do not shout at the client. Shout at the filing cabinet; it is used to neglect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The firm is not a boat, but I admire your commitment to damp management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You care loudly. We will translate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: When you say all hands on deck, Asia hears all unpaid labour to reception.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I mock you because you keep deserving it and occasionally deserve protection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your panic is not useless. It alerts the building that law is occurring.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No, a ship metaphor will not persuade the judge. Unless the judge is also lost at sea, which I doubt.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You built a ridiculous office with an open door. Try not to ruin that by narrating its drowning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If we survive, you may purchase a decorative anchor labelled 'narrow remedy'.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Mr. Pig, breathe. Maritime language after the order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The firm is absurd. It is also necessary. Try saying the second part without the foghorn.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your heart is in the right place. Your metaphors are in international waters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Swine, your charm has returned wearing invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Every time you say informally, a liability grows tiny legs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Personally is a lovely word until the firm receives the bill.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You brought gifts, receipts, and a narrative that needs adult supervision.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The mountain did not make you sign. The pudding did not either.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I am trying to be fair, but your scarf is testifying against you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You hide generosity like a man hiding a fire in a paper archive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You did good badly. That is still a problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The fact that your chaos had a conscience is inconveniently moving.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If you had told Asia, the ledger would have been indexed and half the tragedy would have lost its costume.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not mistake forgiveness for the absence of accounting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: I will defend the purpose and insult the method.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That seems balanced.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Your postcards have caused more dread than several pleadings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: No more truth by scenery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Waldek, they are trying to make your home sound like a window management issue. We will not let them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: A poster is paper. A home is morning light, shoes by the door, and a kettle that knows your hand.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You do not need to sound brave for the file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Tell us what leaving would take from you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The law sometimes forgets that a flat is a life with walls. We will remind it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not apologise for being frightened. The notice was designed to frighten.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: They call it compliance. We ask whether compliance needed a person to lose a home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You are not the poster case. You are Waldek.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: If you cannot sleep because of a letter, the letter has already changed your home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: We will keep the words simple. They have done enough violence already.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The court must see the room before it reads the rule.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: You brought the fear. We will bring the structure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: And if I become too poetic, Crab will throw a date at me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: That is our system.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: It works more often than it should.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: The printer has been framed. I knew it had tragic depth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Small tray, large soul. Yes, Bajtek, I know it is a machine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Whimsy
text: Do not accuse the printer again until it has counsel and toner.
